behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watcher behavior (recursive watch + OS event delivery), not just local parsing logic. MOCK-MISS/REAL-INFRA: this behavior is about an actual file being placed under the watched tree and being observed by the running daemon. MOCK-HIDE: a mocked watcher can trivially emit the expected event and miss real recursive-watch misconfiguration. Agentic/Workflow rejected because the behavior text contains no AI/ML dependency and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temp input/output directory. Create a nested subdirectory under the watched root, write a file into it, and assert the transformed output appears once in the output directory. This uses real OS file events to catch non-recursive watch failures.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text targets local decision logic: "deduplication logic" and "same file path within a configurable time window." MOCK-MISS: this can be reproduced by calling the dedup component twice with controlled time. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text, (b) no AI/ML output dependency, (c) no multi-step agent chain. Rejected higher levels: Integration/System are disqualified by the behavior focusing on path+window rule, not watcher/OS semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the dedup module with a fake/controlled clock, submit request(path) twice within the configured window, assert first is accepted and second is rejected; then advance clock past window and assert acceptance resumes.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly names OS multi-event emission. REAL-INFRA: this failure appears at OS watcher boundary. MOCK-HIDE: synthetic single-event mocks can pass while real watchers emit duplicate create/change events. Agentic/Workflow rejected because no AI/ML and no multi-agent sequence in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with a real watched directory. Perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., create+append/rename) and verify exactly one pipeline execution/output for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is long-running watcher resilience to real directory deletion/recreation events. REAL-INFRA: requires OS filesystem primitives and watcher re-subscription behavior. MOCK-HIDE: mocked events often bypass actual watch-handle invalidation edge cases. Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text has no AI/ML dependency and no agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching a real directory. Delete the watched directory, recreate it at the same path, then add a new file and assert it is processed without restarting the daemon process.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Although ordering logic can be unit-tested, this behavior depends on real arrival concurrency plus filesystem timestamp semantics used by the daemon. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes may not surface race/timestamp-resolution issues seen with real FS events and stat metadata. Agentic/Workflow rejected because no AI/ML output dependence and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running on real directories, create several files in rapid succession with controlled creation times (or staggered writes to enforce distinct timestamps), then assert processing order matches filesystem creation timestamp order observed by the daemon.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears in real write-in-progress behavior (partial writes, event timing, file stability checks) at OS/filesystem boundary. MOCK-HIDE: mocked "file complete" states can miss truncation/partial-read races that happen with real I/O timing. Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text contains no AI/ML dependency and no agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon, begin writing a large file in chunks with delays so arrival event occurs before completion, and verify daemon does not process until final chunk/close completes; assert output reflects full file content only once.
